nodes.label: cargo-dist Release Tooling
nodes.rationale: Autogenerated by axodotdev's cargo-dist: checks for a release-looking git tag, builds archives/installers/hashes per platform, and undrafts a pre-existing draft GitHub Release with the artifacts.

nodes.label: STL Asset Support
nodes.rationale: Highlight of release 0.5.0: STL files usable as the cursor model or as inline objects, with an optional texture applied from config for both obj and stl models.

nodes.label: Contribution Guide
nodes.rationale: Keeps changes easy to review and practical to integrate; larger or breaking changes should start as an issue so direction is discussed before implementation.

nodes.label: Security Policy
nodes.rationale: Placeholder GitHub template: declares 0.x.x as supported but the vulnerability reporting instructions are unfilled boilerplate.

nodes.label: Ratty Terminal Emulator
nodes.rationale: A GPU-rendered terminal emulator with inline 3D graphics, inspired by TempleOS and built with Rust and Ratatui; features a spinning rat cursor, 2D and 3D modes, inline 3D objects, and Kitty-protocol image support.

nodes.label: 3D Mode
nodes.rationale: Toggled with Ctrl+Alt+Enter to reveal the scene behind the terminal surface; warp amount is adjustable and a Mobius mode variant exists.

nodes.label: Spinning Rat Cursor
nodes.rationale: Customizable 3D cursor model (cursor.model in ratty.toml) supporting .obj, .glb and .stl assets with scale_factor, brightness, x_offset, plane_offset, visibility and spin/bob animation settings.

nodes.label: GPU Rendering Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Terminal image stays fully GPU-resident: PTY output parsed by vt100 into a Ratatui buffer on CPU, shaped by parley_ratatui into a Vello scene, handed to Bevy's render world through a double-buffered exchange (scenes recycled, never cloned or re-recorded); Vello renders into an Rgba8Unorm storage texture with no CPU readback, then it is copied to an Rgba8UnormSrgb present texture sampled through an sRGB view so Vello's sRGB-encoded output is decoded on sample instead of re-encoded. Only the recorded scene crosses from main world to render world each frame, not pixels.

nodes.label: Kitty Graphics Protocol Image Support
nodes.rationale: Ratty renders inline images via the Kitty Graphics Protocol (experimental), separate from its own RGP for 3D objects.

nodes.label: Ratty Nix Flake
nodes.rationale: Provides a Nix package for Ratty across x86_64/aarch64 Linux and Darwin, plus NixOS and Home Manager modules and a dev shell; maintained by DarthPJB.

nodes.label: NixOS Module (programs.ratty)
nodes.rationale: Installs Ratty system-wide, writes /etc/ratty/ratty.toml only when settings is non-empty, and wraps the binary with --config-file and GPU env vars; a single wrapper applies all flags when both settings and GPU options are set.

nodes.label: Home Manager Module (programs.ratty)
nodes.rationale: User-level configuration without root: writes $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/ratty/ratty.toml and applies GPU options via home.sessionVariables instead of a binary wrapper.

nodes.label: GPU Backend Selection (gpuBackend/gpuAdapter)
nodes.rationale: Workaround for systems with multiple GPUs or where default Vulkan device creation fails (e.g. NVIDIA 580.x drivers reporting unsupported features): sets WGPU_BACKEND (vulkan/gl/gles) and WGPU_ADAPTER_NAME (substring match) to control wgpu device selection.

nodes.label: Standalone Nix Package (nix/default.nix)
nodes.rationale: Deliberately takes only standard nixpkgs arguments with no flake-specific constructs so it can be upstreamed to nixpkgs as pkgs/by-name/ra/ratty/package.nix.

nodes.label: Ratty Graphics Protocol (RGP)
nodes.rationale: Custom terminal protocol that attaches a semantic 3D graphics object to terminal cells so it becomes part of the terminal surface rather than an external overlay. Design goals: support 3D assets directly, anchor objects to cell positions, make graphics native terminal objects, and allow future interactive behavior (clicking, macros, object state updates). Foundation for 'sprites on the command line'.

nodes.label: APC Escape Sequence Transport
nodes.rationale: RGP rides on APC (Application Program Command): ESC _ ratty;g;<verb>[;key=value...] ESC \, where ratty is the namespace and g means graphics.

nodes.label: Support Query Verb (s)
nodes.rationale: Feature detection: terminal replies with version and capability flags (fmt, path, payload, chunk, anim, depth, color, brightness, transform, update, normalize); no reply means no protocol support.

nodes.label: Register Object Asset Verb (r)
nodes.rationale: Registers a 3D object by application-chosen id with fmt obj/glb/stl; supports path-based and payload-based sources additively, plus an OBJ normalize flag (center on bounding-box center, scale by largest axis) defaulting to 1.

nodes.label: Place Object Verb (p)
nodes.rationale: Places a registered object into terminal cell space anchored at row/col spanning w x h cells, with optional animate, scale, depth, color, brightness, translation (px/py/pz), rotation (rx/ry/rz) and non-uniform scale (sx/sy/sz); v1-only clients keep working unchanged.

nodes.label: Update Object Verb (u)
nodes.rationale: Updates styling or transform of a placed object in-place without changing its registration or anchor; fields mirror the mutable fields from the place verb.

nodes.label: Delete Object Verb (d)
nodes.rationale: Deletes one object by id, or all Ratty graphics objects when id is omitted.

nodes.label: Payload-based Registration
nodes.rationale: Exists for cases such as SSH where the sending application cannot rely on a shared filesystem path on the terminal side: base64 asset bytes are embedded in the register command (source=payload) and may be split into chunks accumulated per id via the more flag until more=0 finalizes registration.

nodes.label: Inline Terminal Object Model
nodes.rationale: Each protocol object has an object id, an anchor cell, a cell span, a renderable payload, and optional metadata reserved for future interaction.

nodes.label: Ratty Website Landing Page
nodes.rationale: Hero video slider of five demos (main demo, 3D mode, draw, TempleOS-style document, big rat) with links to the introducing-ratty blog post, GitHub releases download, and source.

nodes.label: ratatui-ratty Widget Crate
nodes.rationale: A ratatui widget for placing inline 3D objects in Ratty: it emits RGP APC sequences into the target buffer cell, and Ratty resolves the asset and renders it anchored to that terminal region. (Referred to as ratatui-rgp in the root README.)

nodes.label: RattyGraphic
nodes.rationale: Widget type wrapping RattyGraphicSettings; register() registers an asset by path, register_payload() registers in-memory bytes, render() places into a buffer region, and update() mutates transform/style in place after placement.

nodes.label: RattyGraphicSettings
nodes.rationale: Builder-style settings (id, format, animate, scale, depth, rotation, brightness) mirroring RGP placement fields.

nodes.label: ObjectFormat
nodes.rationale: Enum of supported asset formats (Obj, Glb, and per RGP v1 also stl) selecting the fmt field of register commands.

nodes.label: Big Rat Example
nodes.rationale: Minimal inline object demo: places a single oversized rat directly in the terminal (widget/examples/big_rat.rs).

nodes.label: Document Example
nodes.rationale: TempleOS-inspired document demo with editable text and embedded inline 3D objects (widget/examples/document.rs).

nodes.label: Draw Example
nodes.rationale: Split-pane drawing demo: 2D canvas on the left with a live 3D preview on the right (widget/examples/draw.rs).

nodes.label: Rubik's Cube Example
nodes.rationale: Interactive 3D Rubik's cube demo (widget/examples/rubiks_cube.rs); highlight of release 0.4.2.

nodes.label: Apple Touch Icon Role (iOS Home Screen)
nodes.norm_label: apple touch icon role (ios home screen)
